title: [Feature]: Evaluate optional InProgress handling for long sink writes
problem: Long-running sink writes may exceed consumer ACK wait settings if the destination is slow but still making progress. JetStream supports progress-style signals, but adding them carelessly could hide stalled writes or make delivery semantics harder to reason about.
proposal: Evaluate optional InProgress handling for long-running sink writes, document the safety boundary, and split implementation work into separate feature requests. InProgress can help avoid premature server-side redelivery while a sink is still actively working, but it must remain optional, disabled by default, bounded, and advisory only.
users: Operators with large batches or slow destinations, maintainers tuning backpressure and redelivery behavior, and reviewers assessing long-running transaction safety.
delivery_semantics: InProgress signals, if implemented, must be advisory only. Final ACK still happens only after durable sink success. If a sink fails after progress signals, the message must remain eligible for redelivery or DLQ according to policy.
security: Progress logs and metrics must not include payloads, message identifiers, or sensitive metadata by default. Intervals and counts must be bounded to avoid noisy or unbounded behavior.
acceptance: Research documents when InProgress is appropriate and when it is unsafe.
Research confirms InProgress must not replace final ACK, NAK, Term, or DLQ behavior.
Recommended implementation work is split into separate feature requests.
Documentation explains deterministic shutdown and low-cardinality metric requirements.
The evaluation issue is closed with public findings and test evidence.
tests: Run backlog manifest validation, Markdown link checks, and scripts/check.sh after the evaluation documentation and follow-up backlog items are in place.
documentation: Update docs/in-progress-evaluation.md, docs/commit-then-ack.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/operations.md, docs/metrics.md, docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, ROADMAP.md, docs/roadmap.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close with documented decision evidence after the evaluation findings are posted and follow-up feature requests are created.
